characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The lovable main character of the show. Advice Backfire: What usually causes his problems in most episodes. Alien Blood: He states in one episode that he has green blood. An Alien Named "Bob": An alien named Gordon. Although he prefers to be called ALF. Aliens Are Bastards: Whenever he acts aggressive.

characters.name: ALF
characters.description: The lovable main character of the show. Advice Backfire: What usually causes his problems in most episodes. Alien Blood: He states in one episode that he has green blood. An Alien Named "Bob": An alien named Gordon. Although he prefers to be called ALF. Aliens Are Bastards: Whenever he acts aggressive.

characters.name: Willie Tanner
characters.description: The often-frazzled head of the Tanner clan. Be Careful What You Wish For: Willie is excited and practically geeks out when he first sees ALF, saying he’s always wanted to meet an alien. He gets over that very quickly. Beware the Nice Ones: When pissed off he can get very intimidating. Bumbling Dad: Zig-Zagged. Willie is generally pretty competent, but having to cover for Alf tends to make him look stupid to others. Butt-Monkey: Usually the one who suffers from Alf's mischief. It's common to see Willie have his dinner stolen by Alf or get forced to pay hefty bills for Alf's TV watching and clumsy damage to the house.

characters.name: Kate Tanner
characters.description: The often-irritated (by ALF) mother of the Tanner clan. Cloudcuckoolander's Minder: She shares this role with Willie for trying to keep ALF out of trouble or mischief. Unfortunately, she's not very good at it either. Deadpan Snarker: Always delivers at least one sarcastic quip per episode. Everyone Has Standards: She was the one most against ALF staying with the family, but upon learning what ghastly treatment would await him in government custody, she feigned ignorance when questioned by a federal agent. Fiery Redhead: When she gets in an angry mood.

characters.name: Lynn Tanner
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: She has her moments. Deadpan Snarker: As expected of a teenage daughter character. '80s Hair: Her style is outdated by today's standards. Jerkass Ball: In the season two episode "Varsity Drag" and the season three episode "Promises, Promises". Lynn becomes quite bratty and pouty in the formernoteBecause Willie and Kate couldn't afford to send her to the college she wanted.and downright hostile with ALF and Brian in the latter.noteBecause ALF let it slip that she was dating an older guy; which led to Kate and Willie grounding Lynn and her having a major falling out with ALF. Plucky Girl: Always the optimist of the family.

characters.name: Brian Tanner
characters.description: The Tanner's middle child and ALF's closest friend on Earth. Cheerful Child: His defining characteristic. Children Are Innocent: By far Alf's biggest defender in the family, taking to him from the get-go and consistently seeing the best in him. Alf frequently spares Brian his usual biting comments out of respect for this innocence. Flat Character: Receives the least character development out of every character. Morality Pet: For Alf.Brian getting upset over thinking that ALF ate Lucky makes ALF realize the morality of killing the family pet.

characters.name: Lucky
characters.description: The Tanner family cat...and constant target of ALF's appetite. Lost Pet Grievance: In the episode, "Live and Let Die". That Poor Cat: Always chased by Alf.

characters.name: Raquel Ochmonek
characters.description: The Tanner's obnoxiously nosy neighbor. Awful Wedded Life: Watching the Ockmoneks is like reading aThe Lockhornscomic. Badly Battered Babysitter: Becomes one in an early episode...not due to anything Bryan does (Bryan's asleep the whole time) and none of it is physical but due to hearing ALF's antics while watchingPsycho(sounds she can't explain) and her husband assuming she's over-reacting to nothing. Bickering Couple, Peaceful Couple: She & Trevor are the bickering one to the Tanner's mostly peaceful one. Evil Old Folks: Averted. She isn't really evil, just really annoying.

characters.name: Trevor Ochmonek
characters.description: Raquel's crass, boorish husband. Awful Wedded Life: Watching the Ockmoneks is like reading aThe Lockhornscomic. Bickering Couple, Peaceful Couple: He & Raquel are the bickering one to the Tanner's mostly peaceful one. Big Applesauce: Where he's from and has the "Noo Yawk" accent and attitude to match. Big Rotten Apple: Why he moved away from New York City.

characters.name: Jake Ochmonek
characters.description: Trevor's nephew, who moves in with them after his father is arrested. Abhorrent Admirer: Often makes passes at Lynn, much to her annoyance. Mr. Fixit: He's very good at fixing things because his father taught him how while they were getting by finding things in the trash to repair and sell. He even boasts that his motto is "If I can't fix it, it ain't broke". Secret-Keeper: Becomes one to ALF after ALF catches him breaking into the Tanner's garage and intimidates him into not telling anybody, though they quickly become friends afterward. What Happened to the Mouse?: Despite being a prominent side character in Season 2 and 3, he disappears in the final season is never mentioned or even brought up over where he is at.

characters.name: Dorothy Halligan
characters.description: Kate's initially overbearing mother. Hypocrite: In her first appearance, she is irate that ALF seems to have taken over the Tanner's house, even though she was doing the exact same thing up until ALF got fed up with hiding and made himself known to her. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: She is very rude to ALF, but she loves her daughter and her family. She also comes to care about ALF, even coming to the Surprise Party to cheer him up when he feels lonely, and being on the verge of crying when he calls her one of his friends. My Beloved Smother: In her debut episode she takes over Kate's life. She grows out of this thanks to Alf. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: To ALF, who sees her the same way (most of the time).
